codeReview.summary: The feature is solidly implemented. SuggestionsPanel.tsx (315 lines) covers all five required behaviors: (1) POSTs serialized Datalog to /v2/suggest/:engine on query state change, (2) renders suggestions as clickable chips grouped by category with CATEGORY_ORDER priority, (3) onSuggestionSelect callback fires with text and category, (4) loading spinner during first fetch plus 'updating…' header text during re-fetch, (5) 300ms debounce (configurable). The fix commit (b51a9559) correctly guards sortedCategories() against non-array values in the API response. Layout.tsx properly wires onSuggestionSelect to model.addPredicate(). Tests are comprehensive: 17 cases in SuggestionsPanel.test.tsx covering all required states. One non-blocking bug found: the finally block in fetchSuggestions always calls setLoading(false) even when the request was aborted, which can prematurely hide the loading indicator while a subsequent request is still in-flight.

issues.description: The finally block calls setLoading(false) unconditionally. When request A is aborted because request B has started, the finally block of the aborted A still fires (JavaScript always runs finally even after a return in catch). This sets loading to false while B is still in-flight, causing the loading spinner / 'updating…' header to disappear prematurely until B's state updates arrive. Fix: use a ref to track the current fetch sequence number (or check controller.signal.aborted before calling setLoading(false) in finally). No test exercises rapid consecutive requests, so this is not caught.

handleSuggestionSelect is declared as (suggestion: string) => void but SuggestionsPanelProps.onSuggestionSelect is typed as (suggestion: string, category: string) => void. TypeScript accepts this because callbacks with fewer parameters are assignable to callbacks with more parameters (structural subtyping). This is not a bug, but it means the category parameter is silently ignored. A comment noting this is intentional (the feature description says 'Clicking a suggestion chip fires an onSuggestionSelect callback that adds the suggestion to the query at the appropriate position', so category-based routing was expected) would be helpful.

Minor: the cast `body.data as Record<string, string[]>` is redundant — body is already typed as SuggestionsResponse where data is Record<string, string[]>. The cast is harmless but slightly misleading as it looks defensive when it is not. The actual defensive guard (Array.isArray check) is in sortedCategories(), which is correct.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The fullstack-worker skill says 'Backend first' but this feature was purely frontend (backend existed from backend-suggestions-api). The worker noted this in skillFeedback.suggestedChanges. The skill could be improved to include a 'verify existing implementation' step before writing tests, to handle features where preconditions have already been fulfilled.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Handoff skillFeedback.suggestedChanges: 'The skill procedure suggests Backend first but this feature was purely frontend (backend was completed in a prior feature).'

sharedStateObservations.observation: The feature added a debounceMs prop to SuggestionsPanel that defaults to 300ms in production but is set to 0 in most tests to avoid timer complexity. This test-seam pattern (configurable timing for testability) is effective but not documented in AGENTS.md. Future workers may independently discover or re-invent the same pattern.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: SuggestionsPanel.tsx:43-48 (debounceMs prop), SuggestionsPanel.test.tsx:71 (renderWithControls defaults debounceMs=0). AGENTS.md has no mention of this pattern.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The defensive Array.isArray guard in sortedCategories() was added after discovering that the /v2/suggest/:engine API response may include non-array fields (e.g. a top-level 'message' string field alongside the category arrays). This API contract quirk is not documented in architecture.md or AGENTS.md. Future workers consuming the suggest endpoint should know that the response data object may contain scalar fields mixed with category arrays.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: commit b51a9559 diff: 'Only array values are included (non-array fields like message are skipped).' SuggestionsResponse type in SuggestionsPanel.tsx:28 has message?: string as a separate field, but the backend may also embed it in data.

summary: PASS. The SuggestionsPanel component is fully implemented and all 17 tests pass (103 total across the suite). All five required behaviors are present: debounced POST to /v2/suggest/:engine on query change, category-grouped chips, onSuggestionSelect callback, loading spinner, and empty/error states. One non-blocking bug exists: the finally block in fetchSuggestions calls setLoading(false) even when the request was aborted (return in catch does not prevent finally), which can prematurely clear the loading indicator during rapid successive queries. This is not caught by any test. Two other minor observations: the category parameter is silently ignored in Layout.tsx's handleSuggestionSelect (TypeScript allows it but it deviates from the feature spec's intent), and a redundant type cast in the success path. None of these block shipping.
